name: Rescorla-Wagner Prediction-Error Agent
description: A discrete trial-by-trial associative learning agent based on the Rescorla-Wagner (1972) error-correction model. The agent maintains associative strengths (V) between spatial cues (grid cell features like nearby food positions) and food outcomes. Learning is driven by prediction error (delta = lambda - sum_V), which shrinks as predictions improve, reproducing acquisition, extinction, and blocking. A motivational hunger state gates both prediction error magnitude and action-value computation. Action selection uses softmax over hunger-modulated appetitive values of neighbouring cells.

variables.name: associative_strengths
variables.description: Dictionary mapping cue identifiers (e.g., cell coordinates or resource-type keys) to learned associative weights toward food outcome. Each V_i represents how strongly cue i predicts food.

variables.name: prediction_error
variables.description: Discrepancy between actual outcome (lambda, modulated by hunger) and summed prediction (V_hat). Positive delta drives acquisition, negative drives extinction.

variables.name: predicted_appetitive_value
variables.description: Summed associative strength of all cues present at the current cell: V_hat(s) = sum(V_i for i in cues(s))

variables.name: outcome_magnitude
variables.description: Actual outcome: 1.0 if food was consumed (US present), 0.0 otherwise (US absent / extinction trial)

variables.name: hunger
variables.description: Internal drive state representing caloric deficit. Rises each step by kappa, drops by eta_sat when food is eaten. Modulates effective prediction error and action values.

variables.name: action_appetitive_values
variables.description: Hunger-modulated appetitive value for each candidate action/cell: Q(s') = H^phi * V_hat(s')

parameters.name: us_learning_rate_extinction
parameters.source: Rescorla & Wagner (1972); extinction is typically slower

parameters.name: hunger_modulation_exponent
parameters.source: Researchgate (2017); motivational gating of prediction error

rules.description: Cue identification: Each grid cell generates cues based on its coordinates and presence of nearby resources. Cues are string keys like 'cell_x_y' or 'food_nearby_dx_dy'.
rules.pseudocode: cues_at_cell = ['cell_' + str(sx) + '_' + str(sy)]; for each food in perception.resources.food: if abs(food.x - sx) <= 1 and abs(food.y - sy) <= 1: cues_at_cell.append('food_near_' + str(food.x) + '_' + str(food.y))

rules.description: Predicted appetitive value at a cell: sum of associative strengths for all cues present
rules.pseudocode: V_hat_s = sum(V.get(cue, 0.0) for cue in cues_at_cell(s))

rules.description: Prediction error: motivationally gated discrepancy between outcome and prediction
rules.pseudocode: lam = 1.0 if ate_food else 0.0; delta = (H ** phi) * lam - V_hat_s

rules.description: Associative strength update (Rescorla-Wagner rule): each cue present at the outcome cell is updated by prediction error scaled by learning rates
rules.pseudocode: beta = beta_plus if lam == 1.0 else beta_minus; for cue in cues_at_cell(s_current): V[cue] = V.get(cue, 0.0) + alpha * beta * delta; V[cue] = clip(V[cue], -1.0, 1.0)

rules.description: Hunger dynamics: hunger increases each step, decreases upon eating, clamped to [0,1]
rules.pseudocode: ate = 1.0 if ate_food else 0.0; H = min(1.0, max(0.0, H + kappa - eta_sat * ate))

rules.description: Appetitive action-value: hunger-modulated predicted value at the destination cell for each action
Softmax action selection over appetitive values
rules.pseudocode: probs[a] = exp(Q[a] / tau) / sum(exp(Q[a_prime] / tau) for a_prime in actions); return sample(actions, probs)

decision_logic.description: Agent perceives current and neighbouring cells, computes cue-based appetitive values modulated by hunger, selects actions via softmax. Eat action is only viable when food is at the current cell.
pseudocode: # 1. Build cue sets for current cell and each neighbour
for each action in [move_up, move_down, move_left, move_right, stay]:
dest = destination_cell(action, perception.x, perception.y, grid_width, grid_height)
V_hat_dest = sum(V.get(cue, 0.0) for cue in cues)
food_here = any(f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y)
V_hat_here = sum(V.get(cue, 0.0) for cue in cues_here)
# Boost eat value slightly to prefer consuming when at food
exp_q = {a: exp((Q[a] - max_q) / tau) for a in Q}
probs = {a: exp_q[a] / total for a in exp_q}

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception['resources']['food']  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_at_current_cell: [f for f in perception['resources']['food'] if f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y']]
perception_to_variables.ate_food: last_action_result gets checked: if action was 'eat' and reward > 0 then lam=1.0 else lam=0.0

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action -> ConsumeEffect reward=1.0 when food is present; movement and stay yield reward=0.0

expected_behaviors.description: Acquisition: associative strengths for cues near food locations increase across trials
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: run 200 steps with stable food positions -> assert V[food_adjacent_cues] > 0.3

expected_behaviors.description: Extinction: when food is removed from a location, associative strengths for cues at that location decay toward zero
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: train 100 steps with food at (3,3), then remove food -> after 100 more steps assert V[cues_at_3_3] < 0.1

expected_behaviors.description: Hunger modulation: agent seeks food more actively when hunger is high
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: compare action entropy at H=0.9 vs H=0.1 -> assert more directed movement (lower entropy) at high hunger

expected_behaviors.description: Conditioned approach: agent preferentially moves toward cells with high associative value
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: after training, place agent equidistant from trained and untrained locations -> assert agent moves toward trained location > 70% of trials

expected_behaviors.description: Prediction error diminishes: delta decreases across repeated successful eating trials at the same location
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: log delta across 50 eat events at same location -> assert delta[last_10].mean < delta[first_10].mean

references: Rescorla, R.A. & Wagner, A.R. (1972) — A Theory of Pavlovian Conditioning: Variations in the Effectiveness of Reinforcement and Nonreinforcement
Researchgate (2017) — Motivational State Controls the Prediction Error in Pavlovian Appetitive-Aversive Interactions
PMC3702630 (2013) — Extinction of Goal-Tracking Also Eliminates the Conditioned Appetitive Response
